what_it_does: Reads .build/rg10/reconciled.json and design/register.json, filters to entries with class=='conflict' (one address described differently by multiple mockups), and deterministically picks one variant per conflict using an ordered heuristic chain (H1 dedicated-screen, H2 feature-reality, H3 richness, H4 first-order fallback). Demotes any 'built' grounding whose maps_to_feature isn't a real feature id to 'proposed'. Writes a draft-resolutions artifact to .build/rg10/conflict-drafts.json with picked/rejected variants, the named deciding heuristic, and a needs_eyes flag for H4 fallbacks.
observations: Heuristics are documented in the docstring in precedence order and each output names which heuristic decided it.
H1 matches by checking whether any address segment (after stripping 'ui://' and splitting on '/') appears as a substring in the variant's mockup filename (lowercased).
H2 returns a tuple (real, built_ok) where built_ok is True unless grounding=='built' on a non-real feature.
H3 (rich) sums lengths of dossier.howto.what and howto.what_you_can_do; H3 only fires when the top is >1.5x the runner-up.
Line 60 contains a conditional-expression-as-condition: `if rich(ranked[0]) > rich(ranked[1]) * 1.5 if len(ranked) > 1 else False:` — evaluates the ternary first, then uses it as the if test.
Final demotion guard (lines 67-68) forces grounding='proposed' for a 'built' claim on a non-real feature, so a false 'built' is never silently kept.
Output summary tallies counts by heuristic (split on ':' prefix) and counts needs_eyes.
Docstring frames this as 'PROPOSES an artifact only' and 'Tim scans and vetoes' — deterministic judgment, human override.
standouts: Each resolution carries the named heuristic that decided it, so the reviewer sees WHY a variant was picked, not just THAT it was.
The H4 fallback explicitly self-flags as needs_eyes ('flagged for your eyes especially') rather than pretending the heuristic resolved it.
The 'never silently keep a false built' demotion enforces a truthfulness invariant on grounding.
conventions: Follows the family pattern: os.chdir to repo root, read/write JSON under .build/rg10/, deterministic 'PROPOSES only' framing, json.dump with indent.
Inline nested-function heuristics (h1/h2/rich) defined per-conflict inside the loop.
Docstring carries the design rationale and the heuristic legend rather than a separate spec.
concerns: Line 60's ternary-as-condition is hard to read and fragile; the IndexError guard is folded into the ternary's else branch — a maintainer could easily misread precedence.
Line 56 indexes v['maps_to_feature'] directly (not .get) when comparing dedicated variants, which would KeyError if a dedicated variant lacks that key, unlike the .get usage elsewhere.
H1 substring matching ('seg in stem') could match unintended addresses if a short segment is a substring of an unrelated filename token.
notes: Deterministic conflict-resolver for cross-mockup registry disagreements: picks one variant per conflict by a named heuristic chain and proposes drafts for Tim to veto, never writing the registry itself.
questions: What is the schema of a 'conflict' entry's variants list (which keys are guaranteed present vs optional)?
Is design/register.json's features list the same real-id source used by the rest of the RG10 family (refcheck)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring says it produces DRAFT resolutions for the 33 cross-screen conflicts via named heuristics and proposes an artifact only, which is exactly what the code does.
apparent_themes: Named/auditable decisions (every pick carries its reason)
Truthfulness invariants on grounding claims (no false 'built')
Refine-before-gating (prepare option-1 ahead so Tim scans a smaller set)
intention_signals: Reduce a 33-way human review burden to a scannable set of pre-resolved drafts where the machine's reasoning is explicit and overridable, so Tim approves/vetoes rather than adjudicates from scratch.
novelty: The explicit precedence-ordered heuristic chain where the chosen heuristic name is emitted alongside each pick, plus self-flagging of the unresolved (H4) cases as needing eyes.
contribution: Contributes a deterministic, auditable conflict-resolution heuristic for reconciling multiple mockups' competing descriptions of one address, with built-truthfulness demotion and a needs-eyes escape hatch.
summary_for_embedding: A deterministic resolver that picks one variant per cross-mockup registry conflict using an ordered named-heuristic chain (dedicated-screen, feature-reality, richness, order) and writes draft resolutions with reasons for human veto.
intention_for_embedding: To pre-resolve the cross-screen conflicts into a small auditable draft set so the human reviews and vetoes named machine judgments instead of adjudicating each conflict raw.
